title: Prepositions of Movement
objectives: Apply prepositions of movement accurately when writing short descriptions about movement in a park or playground
Show enthusiasm during collaborative sharing of mini stories about movement actions

key_concepts.explanation: Use prepositions of movement to show direction or how someone/something moves from one place to another. Six prepositions: across (from one side to the other, over a surface) — Layla is walking across the bridge. towards (in the direction of something/someone) — Saleh kicked the ball towards the goal. through (from one side to the other, inside a space) — She is walking through the tunnel. into (from outside to inside) — The swimmer jumped into the pool. over (above or higher than) — The ball went over the net. around (in a circular way) — They are running around the cones.

solution_steps: Movement is from one side to the other, over a surface → across
examples.answer: Layla is walking across the bridge.

examples.problem: Saleh kicked the ball ___ the goal.
solution_steps: Movement is in the direction of something → towards
examples.answer: Saleh kicked the ball towards the goal.

solution_steps: Movement from one side to the other, inside a space → through
examples.answer: She is walking through the tunnel.

solution_steps: Movement from outside to inside → into
examples.answer: The swimmer jumped into the pool.

solution_steps: Movement above or higher than → over
examples.answer: The ball went over the net.

solution_steps: Movement in a circular way → around
examples.answer: They are running around the cones.

exercises.problem: Underline the correct answer: 1. The ball flies (at / over) the net. 2. The player runs (under / across) the field. 3. The runner goes (around / into) the cones. 4. The boy moves (between / towards) the goal.

Write a short story of 4-5 sentences about a fun day in a park or playground. Use each preposition of movement at least once: across, towards, through, into, over, around. Underline the prepositions in your story.
exercises.answer: Example: Yesterday, I walked towards the park near my house. I ran across the field and jumped over a small bench. Then, I walked through the trees and climbed into the slide. At the end, I ran around the playground and had a great time.

teaching_notes: Grammar lesson on 6 prepositions of movement: across, towards, through, into, over, around. Grammar Box with table showing preposition, meaning, and example sentence. Think-Pair-Share with movement pictures. Practice: controlled (underline correct) then freer (mini story using all 6 prepositions). Pictures show sports scenarios matching each preposition. 1 page.
